@@ -219,6 +219,12 @@ Data source options of JSON can be set via:
If specified, the entire JSON record is parsed and stored as a single column of VariantType with the given column name, instead of being split into individual fields.

+ If specified, each input file is treated as a single JSON object document with a top-level array-valued field of the given name (e.g. {"Records": [record1, record2, ...]}). Each element of that array becomes one row, stored as a single column of VariantType, and anything outside the array is ignored. This is useful for ingesting formats such as AWS CloudTrail. Mutually exclusive with singleVariantColumn. Because each file is consumed as a whole document, line-based options such as multiLine and lineSep are ignored.
Enabled if the time parser policy has legacy settings or if no custom date or timestamp pattern was provided.

+  // This option takes in a field name and specifies that the input JSON files are object

+  // `{..., "<fieldName>": [element1, element2, ...], ...}`. Each element of the array becomes one

+  // elements are streamed out of the array one at a time, without ever buffering the whole
+  // document; the option intends to make ingestion of formats like AWS CloudTrail
+  // The inferred schema names the variant column after the array field; a user-specified schema

+  // Similar to `singleVariantColumn`; the two options are mutually exclusive.

+ * A [[JsonDataSource]] for the `explodeEmbeddedArray` option. The input JSON files are expected
+ * to be object documents with a top-level array-valued field named by the option, i.e.
+ * records out of the array one at a time, and each record is parsed by the regular JSON parsing
+ * logic as if it were an independent input record, producing one row per record. Neither the
+ * whole array text nor the result rows are ever buffered in memory.
